content.full_text:
People
Pride
Progress
January 16, 1992
Mr. John W. Wallace, Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428"Ju Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, GA 95814
RE: Use of Campaign Funds
Dear Mr. Wallace:
I am presently an elected Councilmember of the City of Moreno Valley with a term
expiring in November of 1992. I expect that I will shortly be filing to announce my intent
to be a candidate for re-election to the same office. After filing my Candidate lntention
Form, I also expect to solicit and receive c€ntributions in support of my campaign for the
office.
ln so far as I am aware, your Commission is presently conducting an investigation into
an allegation that as a City Councilmember, I have violated the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act. I have retained the services of an attorney-at-law
to represent my interests in the Commission's investigation. h is my intent, if permissible
under applicable laws and regulations, and assuming future receipt of campaign
contributions, to use some amount of such receipts to defray the cost of that legal
representation. I am presently paying for such services out of other non-campaign funds.
Itherefore request, and will appreciate receiving from you, the Commission's opinion or
formal written advice on the question of whether or not I may use campaign funds to
defray my attorney's fees and other costs of representation arising out of the
Commission's investigation of the above-referenced alleged violation by me of the conflict
of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
Please let me know if you have any questions or if you desire further information
regarding my request.
Very truly yours,
b*rd.-*/
Denise V. Lanning 
&'
Councilwoman, City. of Moreno Valley

sections.question: Whether or not I may use campaign funds to defray my attorney's fees and other costs of representation arising out of the Commission's investigation of the above-referenced alleged violation by me of the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a candidate use campaign funds to pay for legal representation costs related to an FPPC investigation into alleged conflict of interest violations?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an elected official to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section is present. The question is clearly stated in the body of the letter requesting FPPC opinion/formal written advice.

embedding.summary: Councilwoman Denise V. Lanning of Moreno Valley requests FPPC written advice on whether she may use campaign funds to pay her attorney's fees for legal representation in connection with an ongoing FPPC investigation into alleged conflict of interest violations.
